title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Irvington, NJ | $900-$2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Irvington, NJ. Humane, effective solutions for $900-$2,500. Licensed experts serving Irvington homes and businesses. Call today!
intro: Irvington, New Jersey residents facing bat infestations need swift, humane solutions to protect their homes and health. Best Pest Control NYC offers comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services designed specifically for Irvington's diverse housing stock, from historic Victorian homes to modern multi-family buildings. Our certified technicians use proven methods to safely remove bats and prevent their return, with services ranging from $900 to $2,500.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has been serving Irvington and the greater Newark area for years, providing licensed bat removal services that comply with New Jersey wildlife protection laws. Our team understands the unique challenges of Irvington properties and delivers fast, effective, and humane bat exclusion solutions with guaranteed results.
localContext: Irvington's proximity to Branch Brook Park and the mix of older residential properties along Clinton Avenue and Springfield Avenue create ideal conditions for bat roosting in attics, chimneys, and wall voids. Our technicians are familiar with the architectural features common in Irvington homes and know exactly where bats are likely to enter and nest.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Irvington, NJ?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Irvington typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, the number of entry points, and the extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Yes, but it must be done humanely and in compliance with New Jersey wildlife laws. Bats are protected species, so removal must occur during specific seasons (typically avoiding maternity season from May through August). Our licensed professionals ensure all work meets state and federal regulations.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Irvington home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, bat droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around small openings, and seeing bats enter or exit your home at twilight. If you notice any of these signs, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our process includes a thorough inspection to locate all entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, sealing all potential entry points, attic cleanup and sanitization if needed, and a warranty to ensure bats don't return. The entire process typically takes 2-4 weeks to complete properly.
